methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Bright Machines
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Naïfo Technologies is a Series C-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Aymeric Barthes and Gaëtan Sévérac, having raised €54.7M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable, establishing a relevant peer set for same-sector, same/adjacent-stage benchmarking.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, relies on five direct comparables: Exotec (Series D, $2.0B), Bedrock Robotics (Series B, $1.8B), Dexterity (Series B, $1.6B), Hadrian (Series C, $1.6B), and Nimble Robotics (Series C, $1.0B). The median valuation across this set is $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $2.0B. This reflects the observed clustering of Series B and Series C robotics companies around the $1.6B–$1.8B band.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $1.9B (high), centered on $1.6B. This midpoint reflects Naïfo's stage positioning relative to peers with similar capital deployment profiles. Key uncertainties include revenue traction and unit economics relative to Hadrian and Nimble Robotics, which occupy identical stage positioning but at opposite ends of the peer range. The single-method triangulation constrains precision; additional approaches (revenue multiple, DCF) would strengthen conviction in this estimate.
